# Clawable — The Agentic AI Handbook

> An open-source, evidence-based handbook on autonomous AI agents and the Business Operating System era. Two editions: a strategic field report for business leaders (C-level, board, Agent Managers), and a technical architecture guide for builders (CTOs, engineers). Every claim is logged, timestamped, and verifiable against production session files.

The handbook documents the first complete autonomous business cycle run by an external operator (Clawable) against a production B2B SaaS (FlowWink) in April 2026. €1.1 million in risk and revenue exposure was surfaced in a single unprompted pass. A full contract negotiation — inbound inquiry to signed 422,400 SEK two-year deal — was completed autonomously, with mandate-governed escalation, in hours.

## Business Edition — For C-Level Leaders

Strategic field report on autonomous AI agents and the Business Operating System. Evidence-based, production-verified, with sources tagged `validated`, `partial`, or `hypothesis`.

- [Foreword — What this handbook proves and who it is for](https://clawable.org/business/00-foreword)
- [The Second Digital Acceleration — Why this is moving faster than anything before](https://clawable.org/business/01-the-awakening)
- [The Automation Ceiling — Why SaaS + workflows solved the easy 80%](https://clawable.org/business/02-the-automation-ceiling)
- [Live Proof — What an autonomous operator actually did, logged and timestamped](https://clawable.org/business/03-live-proof)
- [The Agent's Superpowers — Four capabilities no workflow will ever have](https://clawable.org/business/04-superpowers)
- [Embedded vs. External — The architectural choice that determines how deep and wide your agent can go](https://clawable.org/business/05-embedded-vs-external)
- [The Skill Audit — What your stack can already do via MCP](https://clawable.org/business/06-skill-audit)
- [Choose Your Operator — Build, buy proprietary, or open-source](https://clawable.org/business/07-choose-operator)
- [The Vendor Agent Trap — Why your SaaS vendor's built-in AI is the most dangerous path](https://clawable.org/business/08-vendor-agent-trap)
- [Why Agent Programs Fail — The six failure modes that end deployments before month six](https://clawable.org/business/09-why-agent-programs-fail)
- [The Enterprise Architecture — Five platforms, five native agents, one coordination layer](https://clawable.org/business/10-external-embedded-hybrid)
- [Enterprise Outlook — MCP readiness map by category, process value map, B2B market 2027](https://clawable.org/business/11-enterprise-outlook)
- [Making SaaS Agent-Ready — What to demand from your vendors](https://clawable.org/business/12-making-saas-agent-ready)
- [Where the World Is Heading — Federation, multi-agent societies, the proliferation roadmap](https://clawable.org/business/13-where-the-world-is-heading)
- [The Mandate Layer — The governance design every autonomous operator requires](https://clawable.org/business/14-the-mandate-layer)
- [Your Role in This — First week, Agent Manager competencies, the jobs question](https://clawable.org/business/15-your-role-in-this)
- [TL;DR — The whole argument in ten minutes](https://clawable.org/business/appendix-tldr)
- [Deployment Checklist — Go-live checklist for an external operator](https://clawable.org/business/appendix-deployment-checklist)
- [Glossary — Operator-edition vocabulary](https://clawable.org/business/appendix-glossary)
- [Sources & Evidence — Primary sources, verification status, evidence labels](https://clawable.org/business/appendix-sources)

## Builder Edition — For CTOs and Engineers

Technical architecture guide for building and operating autonomous agents at scale. Covers OpenClaw internals, FlowWink/FlowPilot architecture, governance, memory, A2A federation, and production patterns.

- [Foreword — Builder edition scope and how to read it](https://clawable.org/builder/00-foreword)
- [Introduction — The architecture of the agentic era](https://clawable.org/builder/01-introduction)
- [Evolution — From assistants to operators](https://clawable.org/builder/02-evolution)
- [Clawable & OpenClaw — The framework and the operator](https://clawable.org/builder/03-clawable-openclaw)
- [OpenClaw Architecture — Identity, heartbeat, memory as files](https://clawable.org/builder/04-openclaw-architecture)
- [Control Plane — How the operator is governed](https://clawable.org/builder/05-control-plane)
- [Claw Ecosystem — The full landscape](https://clawable.org/builder/06-claw-ecosystem)
- [Models & Lifecycle — Model selection, token management](https://clawable.org/builder/07-models-lifecycle)
- [API Layer — Surface design and authentication](https://clawable.org/builder/08-api-layer)
- [FlowWink Laws — The nine laws of agentic system design](https://clawable.org/builder/09-flowwink-laws)
- [Heartbeat Protocol — Scheduled autonomous operation](https://clawable.org/builder/10-heartbeat-protocol)
- [Concurrency & Observability — Production-grade operator infrastructure](https://clawable.org/builder/11-concurrency-observability)
- [MCP Deep Dive — Tool schemas, descriptions, strict validation](https://clawable.org/builder/12-mcp-deep-dive)
- [FlowPilot Architecture — The embedded agent reference implementation](https://clawable.org/builder/14-flowpilot-architecture)
- [Skills Ecosystem — Skill design, trust levels, approval gates](https://clawable.org/builder/15-skills-ecosystem)
- [Memory Architecture — Four-tier memory: session, working, long-term, semantic](https://clawable.org/builder/18-memory-architecture)
- [Stagnation and Drift — The two long-term failure modes of autonomous agents](https://clawable.org/builder/21-stagnation-and-drift)
- [Human-in-the-Loop — Keeping humans in the chain without becoming the bottleneck](https://clawable.org/builder/22-human-in-the-loop)
- [Agent Governance — Accountability, McKinsey four-layer model, compliance](https://clawable.org/builder/23-agent-governance)
- [A2A Communication — Agent-to-agent federation protocol](https://clawable.org/builder/26-a2a-communication)
- [Security — Prompt injection, tool poisoning, SSRF, scope isolation](https://clawable.org/builder/29-security)
- [Testing Agents — OMATS framework, L1–L7 test layers, peer validation](https://clawable.org/builder/30-testing-agents)
- [Resilience Patterns — Retry, idempotency, circuit breakers, self-healing](https://clawable.org/builder/31-resilience-patterns)
- [ClawStack — Production deployment architecture](https://clawable.org/builder/33-clawstack)
- [The Future — Multi-agent societies, agent-native organizations](https://clawable.org/builder/35-the-future)

## Key Concepts

**Business Operating System (BOS)** — The layer above SaaS that reads across all platforms via MCP, reasons about the cross-system state of a business, and acts on what matters. The successor to SaaS as the primary operational layer.

**MCP (Model Context Protocol)** — The open standard that makes SaaS platforms legible to autonomous agents. Anthropic, 2024. Adopted by Salesforce (GA April 2026), HubSpot (production), Microsoft, SAP, ServiceNow, and the broader ecosystem.

**External Operator** — An autonomous agent deployed outside the SaaS platforms it operates. Reads across systems via MCP. Sees the cross-system patterns no individual platform's agent can see.

**Heartbeat** — The scheduled cycle an operator runs autonomously: reads objectives, sweeps the business state, acts on findings, goes back to sleep. Typical cadence: 30 minutes to several hours.

**Mandate Layer** — The governance boundary every autonomous operator requires: what it is authorised to decide alone, and what it must escalate before acting. Defined in `AGENTS.md`.

**Agent Manager** — The human role created by the AI revolution. Leads, develops, and gets results from AI agents. HBR, February 2026.

**FlowWink** — The reference implementation of an agent-native BOS. Open source, self-hosted, 200+ MCP skills across CRM, finance, ERP, content, HR, and accounting. Available at github.com/magnusfroste/flowwink.

**Clawable** — The external OpenClaw operator documented in this handbook. Runs against FlowWink's production MCP surface. All session logs are public and cross-referenced in the sources appendix.
